user_message: [AUTOMATION TRIGGER] Daily automation: Calendar event with non-Microsoft external attendee detected. Account: Iron Creek Mining.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-02-18 14:04 EST
Subject: deployment blockers - Action Required

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Karen Berg <karen.berg@ironcreekmin.com>
Date: 2024-01-22 10:46 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Iron Creek Mining deployment blockers

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our CTO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Iron Creek Mining
Date: 2024-02-18
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft)

[00:01:30] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into deployment blockers.

[00:08:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Let me add some context -- we've been working on cost optimization and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:10:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is integration complexity.

[00:12:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:14:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around technical validation.

[00:17:00] Karen Berg (Iron Creek Mining): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Iron Creek Mining: there's a dependency on the security team's review that could delay us

evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence
